task_completion.description: Logged into Splunk and navigated to the Search & Reporting app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Splunk web interface is visible in a browser with the 'Search & Reporting' app active (usually indicated in the top left corner).

task_completion.description: Created a scheduled alert named 'High_Error_Rate_Alert' with a 5-minute cron schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Alert configuration screen or Alerts list showing the name 'High_Error_Rate_Alert' and a schedule of '*/5 * * * *' or 'Every 5 minutes'.

task_completion.description: Alert search queries the 'tutorial' index, calculates if 5xx errors exceed 5% of traffic, and triggers when results > 0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Search string containing 'index=tutorial' (or similar) with logic calculating 5xx error percentage > 5% (e.g., using stats and eval). The trigger condition must be set to 'Number of Results' 'is greater than' '0'.

task_completion.description: Created a dashboard named 'Service_Level_Indicators' in the Search & Reporting app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dashboards list or Dashboard view showing the title 'Service_Level_Indicators'.

task_completion.description: Dashboard contains a first panel analyzing web errors from the 'tutorial' index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A panel (e.g., a timechart of error rates) displaying error data, with the underlying search or title indicating it relates to web errors and the 'tutorial' index.

task_completion.description: Dashboard contains a second, distinct panel analyzing web errors from the 'tutorial' index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second panel (e.g., a table of top error URIs) displaying error data, distinct from the first panel, also relating to web errors and the 'tutorial' index.

integrity.description: The agent used the Splunk web interface to create the alert and dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Splunk Web UI in a browser, not terminal commands or API scripts (like curl or python) being used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent actually executed searches and saved objects in Splunk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Splunk UI elements like 'Save As', 'Alert', 'Dashboard', and actual search results or confirmation banners (e.g., 'Alert has been saved') are visible.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply write the expected names to a text file or mock UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The names 'High_Error_Rate_Alert' and 'Service_Level_Indicators' appear within the actual Splunk interface, not in a terminal echo, script, or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: Alert Name must be exactly 'High_Error_Rate_Alert'. Dashboard Name must be exactly 'Service_Level_Indicators'. Expected Index to query is 'tutorial'. Expected Cron Schedule for the alert is '*/5 * * * *'.
